title: Atlantic reef ecosystems and sea warming
text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Atlantic reef ecosystems and sea warming
text:
Project description
Coral reefs are among the most diverse and sensitive ecosystems on the planet, with multiple reports of severe impacts by ocean warming.






ImportantOpen questions adressed:




To what extent sea warming influence reef species standing biomass?
Does sea warming change the ecosystem energy transfer efficiency ?
Do species trophic interaction strenght modulate sea warming influence ?



To understand the impacts of ocean warming on reef ecosystems, we investigated the potential future effects of human-induced ocean warming on standing biomass in reef food webs using a near-pristine tropical reef ecosystem in the Southwestern Atlantic Ocean as a model. We focus on We did in situ experiments (video link here), fish visual censuses and food web modelling to forecast to what extent temperature affects reef fish trophic interactions in the Western Atlantic and modeled projections of changes in reef food web species due to climate change. 



Experiment setup with GoPro camera and the herbivorous fish Acanthurus chirurgus individual in frame at the Rocas Atoll, Western Atlantic. Photographs credits: Dr. Natalia Roos.

title: Atlantic reef ecosystems and sea warming
text:
Results obtained

By the end of this century reef ecosystem standing biomass is projected to decline by approximately 1 %, 8 %, and 44 % under low, moderate, and high warming scenarios.
Ecosystem transfer efficiency will drop by ~2 % and biomass residence time will decrease by ~10 % under the high warming scenario.
The result is a simplification of reef communities, with fewer resources and lower resilience to disturbance.








Response of total fish standing biomass to ocean warming. Retrieved from [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Atlantic reef ecosystems and sea warming
text:
Research paper associated:
Capitani, L., de Araujo, J. N., Vieira, E. A., Angelini, R., & Longo, G. O. (2022). Ocean warming will reduce standing biomass in a tropical Western Atlantic reef ecosystem. Ecosystems, 25, 843–857. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z
Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107

title: Ecosistemi dei reef atlantici e riscaldamento marino
text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Ecosistemi dei reef atlantici e riscaldamento marino
text:
Descrizione del progetto
Le barriere coralline sono tra gli ecosistemi più diversi e sensibili del pianeta, con numerose segnalazioni di gravi impatti dovuti al riscaldamento degli oceani.






ImportanteDomande di ricerca affrontate:




In che misura il riscaldamento marino influenza la biomassa in piedi delle specie di barriera?
Il riscaldamento marino modifica l’efficienza di trasferimento energetico dell’ecosistema?
L’intensità delle interazioni trofiche tra le specie modula l’influenza del riscaldamento marino?



Per comprendere gli impatti del riscaldamento oceanico sugli ecosistemi di barriera, abbiamo studiato i potenziali effetti futuri del riscaldamento oceanico di origine antropica sulla biomassa in piedi nelle reti trofiche di barriera, utilizzando come modello un ecosistema di barriera tropicale quasi incontaminato nell’Atlantico sud-occidentale. Abbiamo condotto esperimenti in situ (video disponibile qui), censimenti visivi ittici e modelli di rete trofica per prevedere in che misura la temperatura influisce sulle interazioni trofiche dei pesci di barriera nell’Atlantico occidentale, oltre a proiezioni modellistiche dei cambiamenti nelle specie della rete trofica di barriera dovuti al cambiamento climatico.



Allestimento sperimentale con videocamera GoPro e un individuo del pesce erbivoro Acanthurus chirurgus inquadrato presso l’Atollo delle Rocas, Atlantico occidentale. Crediti fotografici: Dr.ssa Natalia Roos.

title: Ecosistemi dei reef atlantici e riscaldamento marino
text:
Risultati ottenuti

Entro la fine di questo secolo, si prevede che la biomassa in piedi dell’ecosistema di barriera diminuirà di circa l’1%, l’8% e il 44% negli scenari di riscaldamento basso, moderato ed elevato.
L’efficienza di trasferimento dell’ecosistema diminuirà di circa il 2% e il tempo di residenza della biomassa si ridurrà di circa il 10% nello scenario di riscaldamento elevato.
Il risultato è una semplificazione delle comunità di barriera, con minori risorse disponibili e una minore resilienza alle perturbazioni.








Risposta della biomassa ittica totale in piedi al riscaldamento oceanico. Tratto da [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Ecosistemi dei reef atlantici e riscaldamento marino
text:
Articoli di ricerca associati:
Capitani, L., de Araujo, J. N., Vieira, E. A., Angelini, R., & Longo, G. O. (2022). Ocean warming will reduce standing biomass in a tropical Western Atlantic reef ecosystem. Ecosystems, 25, 843–857. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z
Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107

title: Ecosistemas de arrecifes atlánticos y calentamiento del mar
text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Ecosistemas de arrecifes atlánticos y calentamiento del mar
text:
Descripción del proyecto
Los arrecifes de coral se encuentran entre los ecosistemas más diversos y sensibles del planeta, con múltiples informes de impactos severos causados por el calentamiento del océano.






ImportantePreguntas de investigación abordadas:




¿En qué medida el calentamiento marino influye en la biomasa en pie de las especies de arrecife?
¿El calentamiento marino cambia la eficiencia de transferencia de energía del ecosistema?
¿La intensidad de la interacción trófica entre especies modula la influencia del calentamiento marino?



Para comprender los impactos del calentamiento oceánico en los ecosistemas de arrecife, investigamos los efectos futuros potenciales del calentamiento oceánico de origen humano sobre la biomasa en pie en las redes tróficas de arrecife, utilizando como modelo un ecosistema de arrecife tropical casi prístino en el Atlántico suroccidental. Realizamos experimentos in situ (video disponible aquí), censos visuales de peces y modelado de redes tróficas para predecir en qué medida la temperatura afecta las interacciones tróficas de los peces de arrecife en el Atlántico occidental, además de proyecciones modeladas de los cambios en las especies de la red trófica de arrecife debidos al cambio climático.



Montaje experimental con cámara GoPro y un individuo del pez herbívoro Acanthurus chirurgus en el encuadre, en el Atolón de las Rocas, Atlántico occidental. Créditos fotográficos: Dra. Natalia Roos.

title: Ecosistemas de arrecifes atlánticos y calentamiento del mar
text:
Resultados obtenidos

Para finales de este siglo, se proyecta que la biomasa en pie del ecosistema de arrecife disminuya aproximadamente un 1 %, 8 % y 44 % en los escenarios de calentamiento bajo, moderado y alto, respectivamente.
La eficiencia de transferencia del ecosistema caerá aproximadamente un 2 % y el tiempo de residencia de la biomasa disminuirá aproximadamente un 10 % en el escenario de calentamiento alto.
El resultado es una simplificación de las comunidades de arrecife, con menos recursos disponibles y menor resiliencia ante perturbaciones.








Respuesta de la biomasa íctica total en pie al calentamiento oceánico. Obtenido de [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Ecosistemas de arrecifes atlánticos y calentamiento del mar
text:

Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107

title: Open Science
text:
🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch


Commitment to research transparency and open science

“Open Science encompasses the entire research process and the entire research community. It empowers researchers everywhere to share valuable artifacts from each stage of their investigation and increase visibility and collaboration around their work. Every research community and individual has an opportunity to shape the activities and norms that inspire trust in their work. A commitment to Open Science, therefore, is not a pledge to adopt a few specific behaviors, but to advance openness, transparency and reproducibility in daily life as a researcher.” (plos.org/commit-to-openness)

As an ecologist, I believe that sharing data, code, and methods openly is what allows our findings to be checked, reused, and built upon by others. It is also, simply, good scientific practice.


Activities

Since 2021 — Voluntary member of the Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (SORTEE).



Links

SORTEE — Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary Biology
GitHub

“Open Science encompasses the entire research process and the entire research community. It empowers researchers everywhere to share valuable artifacts from each stage of their investigation and increase visibility and collaboration around their work. Every research community and individual has an opportunity to shape the activities and norms that inspire trust in their work. A commitment to Open Science, therefore, is not a pledge to adopt a few specific behaviors, but to advance openness, transparency and reproducibility in daily life as a researcher.” (plos.org/commit-to-openness)

In quanto ecologo, credo che condividere apertamente dati, codice e metodi sia ciò che permette ai nostri risultati di essere verificati, riutilizzati e migliorati da altri. È anche, semplicemente, buona pratica scientifica.

title: Dr. Leonardo Capitani
text:
🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch


Who am I ?
Well, I am an ecologist. What is an ecologist? An ecologist is someone who finds excitement in the beauty and complexity of nature. I am also a passionate educator and thoroughly enjoy chances to teach and learn with audiences of all levels of prior experience.

And so an ecologist who begins by asking himself how life works very quickly finds himself asking instead why the natural ecosystems are made up of so many parts and why there are so many of each kind of part. Before he can answer an engineer’s question “how does this work,” he is faced with even more fundamental questions beginning with “why”: Why are there so many different kinds of plants and animals? Why are some common but others rare? Why are some large but others small? Why do they sometimes do such peculiar things?
Paul Colinvax, 1978. Why big fierce animals are rare? Princeton University Press.





Antonio Ligabue, Volpe in fuga. 1948. Private Collection.



Created by Leonardo Capitani; Updated 15 July 2026



 Back to top

title: Dr. Leonardo Capitani
text:
🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch


Chi sono?
Ebbene, sono un ecologo. Cos’è un ecologo? Un ecologo è qualcuno che trova entusiasmo nella bellezza e nella complessità della natura. Sono anche un educatore appassionato e apprezzo molto le occasioni di insegnare e imparare con pubblici di ogni livello di esperienza.

And so an ecologist who begins by asking himself how life works very quickly finds himself asking instead why the natural ecosystems are made up of so many parts and why there are so many of each kind of part. Before he can answer an engineer’s question “how does this work,” he is faced with even more fundamental questions beginning with “why”: Why are there so many different kinds of plants and animals? Why are some common but others rare? Why are some large but others small? Why do they sometimes do such peculiar things?
Paul Colinvax, 1978. Why big fierce animals are rare? Princeton University Press.





Antonio Ligabue, Volpe in fuga. 1948. Collezione privata.



Creato da Leonardo Capitani; Aggiornato il 15 July 2026

title: Open Science
text:
🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch


Engagement für Forschungstransparenz und Open Science

“Open Science encompasses the entire research process and the entire research community. It empowers researchers everywhere to share valuable artifacts from each stage of their investigation and increase visibility and collaboration around their work. Every research community and individual has an opportunity to shape the activities and norms that inspire trust in their work. A commitment to Open Science, therefore, is not a pledge to adopt a few specific behaviors, but to advance openness, transparency and reproducibility in daily life as a researcher.” (plos.org/commit-to-openness)

Als Ökologe glaube ich, dass das offene Teilen von Daten, Code und Methoden es ermöglicht, unsere Ergebnisse zu überprüfen, wiederzuverwenden und von anderen weiterzuentwickeln. Es ist außerdem schlicht gute wissenschaftliche Praxis.


Aktivitäten

Seit 2021 — Ehrenamtliches Mitglied der Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (SORTEE).

title: Open Science
text:
🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch


Engagement pour la transparence de la recherche et la science ouverte

“Open Science encompasses the entire research process and the entire research community. It empowers researchers everywhere to share valuable artifacts from each stage of their investigation and increase visibility and collaboration around their work. Every research community and individual has an opportunity to shape the activities and norms that inspire trust in their work. A commitment to Open Science, therefore, is not a pledge to adopt a few specific behaviors, but to advance openness, transparency and reproducibility in daily life as a researcher.” (plos.org/commit-to-openness)

En tant qu’écologue, je crois que partager ouvertement les données, le code et les méthodes est ce qui permet à nos résultats d’être vérifiés, réutilisés et enrichis par d’autres. C’est aussi, tout simplement, une bonne pratique scientifique.


Activités

Depuis 2021 — Membre bénévole de la Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (SORTEE).



Liens

SORTEE — Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent Ecology and Evolutionary Biology

Compromisso com a transparência da pesquisa e a Open Science

“Open Science encompasses the entire research process and the entire research community. It empowers researchers everywhere to share valuable artifacts from each stage of their investigation and increase visibility and collaboration around their work. Every research community and individual has an opportunity to shape the activities and norms that inspire trust in their work. A commitment to Open Science, therefore, is not a pledge to adopt a few specific behaviors, but to advance openness, transparency and reproducibility in daily life as a researcher.” (plos.org/commit-to-openness)

Como ecólogo, acredito que compartilhar dados, código e métodos abertamente é o que permite que nossos resultados sejam verificados, reutilizados e ampliados por outras pessoas. Além disso, é simplesmente uma boa prática científica.

text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Atlantische Riffökosysteme und Meereserwärmung
text:
Projektbeschreibung
Korallenriffe zählen zu den vielfältigsten und empfindlichsten Ökosystemen des Planeten, mit zahlreichen Berichten über schwerwiegende Auswirkungen der Ozeanerwärmung.






WichtigBehandelte Forschungsfragen:




Inwieweit beeinflusst die Meereserwärmung die stehende Biomasse von Riffarten?
Verändert die Meereserwärmung die Energieübertragungseffizienz des Ökosystems?
Moduliert die Stärke trophischer Interaktionen zwischen Arten den Einfluss der Meereserwärmung?



Um die Auswirkungen der Ozeanerwärmung auf Riffökosysteme zu verstehen, untersuchten wir die potenziellen zukünftigen Effekte der menschengemachten Ozeanerwärmung auf die stehende Biomasse in Riff-Nahrungsnetzen, wobei ein nahezu unberührtes tropisches Riffökosystem im südwestlichen Atlantik als Modell diente. Wir führten In-situ-Experimente durch (Video hier), visuelle Fischzählungen sowie Nahrungsnetzmodellierungen, um vorherzusagen, inwieweit die Temperatur die trophischen Interaktionen von Riff-Fischen im westlichen Atlantik beeinflusst, einschließlich modellierter Projektionen von Veränderungen der Riff-Nahrungsnetzarten infolge des Klimawandels.



Versuchsaufbau mit GoPro-Kamera und einem Individuum des pflanzenfressenden Fisches Acanthurus chirurgus im Bild, am Rocas-Atoll, westlicher Atlantik. Fotocredits: Dr. Natalia Roos.

title: Atlantische Riffökosysteme und Meereserwärmung
text:
Erzielte Ergebnisse

Bis zum Ende dieses Jahrhunderts wird ein Rückgang der stehenden Biomasse des Riffökosystems um etwa 1 %, 8 % beziehungsweise 44 % bei geringer, moderater und starker Erwärmung prognostiziert.
Die Transfereffizienz des Ökosystems wird im Szenario starker Erwärmung um etwa 2 % sinken, und die Verweilzeit der Biomasse wird um etwa 10 % abnehmen.
Das Ergebnis ist eine Vereinfachung der Riffgemeinschaften mit weniger verfügbaren Ressourcen und geringerer Widerstandsfähigkeit gegenüber Störungen.








Reaktion der gesamten stehenden Fischbiomasse auf die Ozeanerwärmung. Entnommen aus [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Atlantische Riffökosysteme und Meereserwärmung
text:
Zugehörige Forschungsarbeiten:
Capitani, L., de Araujo, J. N., Vieira, E. A., Angelini, R., & Longo, G. O. (2022). Ocean warming will reduce standing biomass in a tropical Western Atlantic reef ecosystem. Ecosystems, 25, 843–857. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z
Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107

title: Écosystèmes récifaux atlantiques et réchauffement marin
text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Écosystèmes récifaux atlantiques et réchauffement marin
text:
Description du projet
Les récifs coralliens comptent parmi les écosystèmes les plus diversifiés et les plus sensibles de la planète, avec de nombreux rapports faisant état d’impacts sévères liés au réchauffement des océans.






ImportantQuestions de recherche abordées :




Dans quelle mesure le réchauffement marin influence-t-il la biomasse sur pied des espèces récifales ?
Le réchauffement marin modifie-t-il l’efficacité de transfert d’énergie de l’écosystème ?
L’intensité des interactions trophiques entre espèces module-t-elle l’influence du réchauffement marin ?



Pour comprendre les impacts du réchauffement océanique sur les écosystèmes récifaux, nous avons étudié les effets futurs potentiels du réchauffement océanique d’origine humaine sur la biomasse sur pied dans les réseaux trophiques récifaux, en utilisant comme modèle un écosystème récifal tropical quasi intact de l’Atlantique sud-ouest. Nous avons réalisé des expériences in situ (vidéo disponible ici), des recensements visuels de poissons et une modélisation des réseaux trophiques afin de prévoir dans quelle mesure la température affecte les interactions trophiques des poissons récifaux dans l’Atlantique occidental, ainsi que des projections modélisées des changements des espèces du réseau trophique récifal liés au changement climatique.



Dispositif expérimental avec caméra GoPro et un individu du poisson herbivore Acanthurus chirurgus dans le champ, à l’atoll das Rocas, Atlantique occidental. Crédits photographiques : Dre Natalia Roos.

title: Écosystèmes récifaux atlantiques et réchauffement marin
text:
Résultats obtenus

D’ici la fin de ce siècle, la biomasse sur pied de l’écosystème récifal devrait diminuer d’environ 1 %, 8 % et 44 % selon les scénarios de réchauffement faible, modéré et élevé.
L’efficacité de transfert de l’écosystème chutera d’environ 2 % et le temps de résidence de la biomasse diminuera d’environ 10 % dans le scénario de réchauffement élevé.
Il en résulte une simplification des communautés récifales, avec moins de ressources disponibles et une résilience réduite face aux perturbations.








Réponse de la biomasse ichtyologique totale sur pied au réchauffement océanique. Tiré de [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Écosystèmes récifaux atlantiques et réchauffement marin
text:
Articles de recherche associés :
Capitani, L., de Araujo, J. N., Vieira, E. A., Angelini, R., & Longo, G. O. (2022). Ocean warming will reduce standing biomass in a tropical Western Atlantic reef ecosystem. Ecosystems, 25, 843–857. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z
Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107

title: Ecossistemas de recifes atlânticos e aquecimento do mar
text: 🌐 English · Italiano · Español · Português · Français · Deutsch

title: Ecossistemas de recifes atlânticos e aquecimento do mar
text:
Descrição do projeto
Os recifes de coral estão entre os ecossistemas mais diversos e sensíveis do planeta, com múltiplos relatos de impactos severos causados pelo aquecimento oceânico.






ImportantePerguntas de pesquisa abordadas:




Em que medida o aquecimento do mar influencia a biomassa em pé das espécies de recife?
O aquecimento do mar altera a eficiência de transferência de energia do ecossistema?
A intensidade da interação trófica entre espécies modula a influência do aquecimento do mar?



Para compreender os impactos do aquecimento oceânico nos ecossistemas de recife, investigamos os efeitos futuros potenciais do aquecimento oceânico de origem antrópica sobre a biomassa em pé nas teias tróficas de recife, utilizando como modelo um ecossistema de recife tropical quase intocado no Atlântico sudoeste. Realizamos experimentos in situ (vídeo disponível aqui), censos visuais de peixes e modelagem de teias tróficas para prever em que medida a temperatura afeta as interações tróficas dos peixes de recife no Atlântico ocidental, além de projeções modeladas das mudanças nas espécies da teia trófica de recife devido às mudanças climáticas.



Montagem experimental com câmera GoPro e um indivíduo do peixe herbívoro Acanthurus chirurgus em quadro, no Atol das Rocas, Atlântico ocidental. Créditos das fotografias: Dra. Natalia Roos.

title: Ecossistemas de recifes atlânticos e aquecimento do mar
text:
Resultados obtidos

Até o final deste século, projeta-se que a biomassa em pé do ecossistema de recife diminua aproximadamente 1 %, 8 % e 44 % nos cenários de aquecimento baixo, moderado e alto, respectivamente.
A eficiência de transferência do ecossistema cairá cerca de 2 % e o tempo de residência da biomassa diminuirá cerca de 10 % no cenário de aquecimento alto.
O resultado é uma simplificação das comunidades de recife, com menos recursos disponíveis e menor resiliência a perturbações.








Resposta da biomassa íctica total em pé ao aquecimento oceânico. Extraído de [Capitani et al. 2022](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10021-021-00691-z).

title: Ecossistemas de recifes atlânticos e aquecimento do mar
text:
Artigos de pesquisa associados:

Capitani, L., Roos, N. C., Longo, G. O., Angelini, R., & Schenone, L. (2021). Resource-to-consumer ratio determines the functional response of an herbivorous fish in a field experiment. Oikos, 130(12), 2100–2110. https://doi.org/10.1111/oik.08784
Artana, C., Capitani, L., Garcia, G. S., & Coll, M. (2024). Food web trophic control modulates tropical Atlantic reef ecosystems response to marine heat wave intensity and duration. Journal of Animal Ecology. https://doi.org/10.1111/1365-2656.14107
